articles.title: Trump Says He Will Order T.S.A. Agents Paid as Funding Deal Stalls
articles.summary: President Trump’s announcement came as talks on Capitol Hill over funding the Department of Homeland Security faltered, and airport lines continued to grow.

articles.title: In Sleepy Town on Strait of Hormuz, War Rages Just Over Horizon
articles.summary: For centuries, an Omani exclave has been defined by a peculiar duality: rugged isolation and proximity to one of the world’s most important trade routes.

articles.title: A surprise resignation could open the door for an independent to win a Montana Senate seat
articles.summary: Two-term GOP Sen. Steve Daines shocked Montana when he announced his retirement. Democrats worry a new independent candidate will split their party's vote.

articles.title: Standoff With Iran Raises Fresh Doubts About Trump’s Freestyle Diplomacy
articles.summary: A jumble of emissaries — a friend, a family member, a dove and a hawk — on the Iran crisis reflects President Trump’s improvisational approach.

articles.title: DOJ admits ICE courthouse arrests relied on erroneous information
articles.summary: Hundreds of immigrants have been arrested at immigration courthouses. It is unclear whether the federal government's admission could lead to some of those arrests being overturned.

articles.title: Trump’s Signature Is Set to Be Added to America’s Currency
articles.summary: President Trump is poised to be the first sitting president to have his signature appear on the U.S. dollar.

articles.title: The depleted Education Department will move out of its headquarters
articles.summary: In August, Education Department employees will relocate to a smaller office roughly a block away, and the larger Energy Department will take over the old headquarters.

articles.title: Trump Officials Investigate Stanford, Ohio State and U.C. San Diego Medical Schools
articles.summary: The Justice Department’s demands for admissions-related data from Stanford, Ohio State and the University of California, San Diego, reveal an expansion of its higher-education pressure campaign.

articles.title: House Ethics Panel Holds Rare Public Hearing on Democrat’s Conduct
articles.summary: Representative Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick of Florida is charged with stealing $5 million in FEMA money for her campaign.

articles.title: Judge temporarily blocks Trump administration's Anthropic ban
articles.summary: The order briefly stops the government from labeling tech company Anthropic a "supply chain risk," calling that "classic First Amendment retaliation."

articles.title: Trump says he'll sign order to pay TSA agents as Congress struggles to reach funding deal
articles.summary: It's an extraordinary move that came as senators were reviewing a "last and final" offer to end the funding impasse that has jammed airports and disrupted travel, just as TSA workers faced another mis

articles.title: What’s on Trump’s Mind During a Cabinet Meeting? Sharpies, Iran and Inflation.
articles.summary: President Trump waxed on about the virtues of the pen, calling it more economical and a better instrument than the fancier writing tools preferred by his predecessors.

articles.title: D.H.S. Funding Lapse Leads to Longest Partial Shutdown in History
articles.summary: If the shutdown continues after this weekend, it will be longer than any previous shutdown, partial or full.

articles.title: Iran's war propaganda includes memes directed at Trump
articles.summary: Iran has been sending not only missiles around the region but also trolling tweets and videos around the internet. It's the latest in global diplomacy.

articles.title: The U.S. and Iran can't even agree on whether they're talking
articles.summary: President Trump says Iran is "begging" for a deal. Iran says it has no intention of negotiating. NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with NPR's Tom Bowman and Aya Batrawy about prospects for an off-ramp.

articles.title: The Olympic committee bans trans athletes from women's events, raising many questions
articles.summary: The International Olympic Committee will require all athletes who want to participate in women's events to undergo genetic testing. The policy takes effect for the 2028 Summer Games in Los Angeles.

articles.title: As TSA agents miss another paycheck, what's happening at airports with private security?
articles.summary: At 20 airports around the U.S., security screeners are getting paid as usual despite the ongoing DHS shutdown — because they're private contractors. Will more airports look at privatizing security?

articles.title: Trump has deployed ICE agents to the nation's airports. What's their role?
articles.summary: The president says ICE agents are being stationed at airports to help reduce long wait times. Here's a look at what they're authorized to do.

articles.title: Southeast Asia turns to nuclear as Iran war disrupts energy supplies
articles.summary: Analysts say the Iran war energy crisis is also adding momentum to nuclear interest and action in the region.

articles.title: Iran rejects U.S. peace plan. And, jury finds Meta, Google to blame in addiction trial
articles.summary: Iran rejects a U.S. proposal to end the war and counters with a different peace plan. And, a jury finds Meta and Google negligent in a trial over social media's harms.

articles.title: Trump grants Iran another extension on a deadline to reopen the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: President Trump said he'll hold off on striking Iran's power plants until April 6, postponing a deadline for Iran to let ships transit safely through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: UN calls for reparations to remedy the 'historical wrongs' of trafficking enslaved Africans
articles.summary: The U.N. General Assembly adopted a resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans "the gravest crime against humanity" and calling for reparations.

articles.title: State-of-the-art air defense systems have not been able to stop some Iranian missiles
articles.summary: The U.S. and Israel say they've depleted most of Iran's missile arsenal, but its weapons — including controversial cluster munitions — are challenging even the most advanced air-defense systems.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
